channel.title: Science, Innovation and Technology Committee — News

item.title: Government agrees to improve tracking of regional innovation
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee today publishes the government’s response to its report, ‘Flying Blind: Innovation, growth and the regions.’

item.title: Belfast unrest: Government have not done enough to tackle online misinformation, committee warns
item.description: The Chair of the Science, Innovation and Technology Chair, Dame Chi Onwurah, has written to the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, Liz Kendall, to warn that the government hasn’t done enough to tackle the amplification of misinformation online.

item.title: MPs warn that Palantir’s increasing presence in the UK public sector is an “unacceptable point of weakness”
item.description: Palantir shouldn’t play such a significant role in the UK public sector, a new report by the Science, Innovation and Technology Committee warns, stressing that it’s not the only company capable of providing the ‘middleware’ required by public bodies.

item.title: Committee endorse appointment of Sir Ian Cheshire as Ofcom chair at critical moment for the online safety regulator
item.description: The cross-party committee has produced a report setting out its endorsement of Sir Cheshire after holding a pre-appointment hearing on Wednesday 20 May.

item.title: ‘The status quo is not acceptable’: Committee responds to social media consultation
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee publishes its response to the government’s consultation on “Growing up in the online world.”

item.title: Biobank data leak: Science, Innovation and Technology Committee responds
item.description: Biobank data leak: Science, Innovation and Technology Committee responds

item.title: Professor Sir Leszek Borysiewicz endorsed as UKRI Chair
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee has endorsed the government’s preferred candidate, Professor Sir Leszek Borysiewicz, as the new chair of UK Research and Innovation (UKRI).

item.title: Science Minister and UKRI CEO give further details of research funding cuts
item.description: Science Minister and UKRI CEO give further details of research funding cuts

item.title: Can low-energy computing help solve the AI energy crisis?
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee today launches a short inquiry examining whether low-energy computing could help tackle the rapidly rising energy demands fuelled by growing AI use.

item.title: Committee presses government and UKRI for urgent clarification on science research funding cuts
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee Chair, Dame Chi Onwurah, has written to the Science Minister, Lord Patrick Vallance, and UKRI Chief Executive, Professor Sir Ian Chapman, seeking further detail on planned cuts to scientific research funding.

item.title: How are digital devices and social media impacting children’s brain development?
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee has today launched a new inquiry examining how digital devices and social media impact the brain development of children and adolescents.

item.title: Government ‘flying blind’ on regional growth without proper data, MPs warn
item.description: Simply pouring money into the existing innovation ecosystem is not enough to facilitate economic growth, MPs warn today. A new report from the Science, Innovation and Technology Committee urges the Government to take a more rigorous, data-driven approach to assess innovation policy.

item.title: UKRI shares first detailed comparisons of past and future spending under new ‘bucket’ model
item.description: Professor Sir Ian Chapman, Chief Executive of UKRI, has written to the Committee Chair, Dame Chi Onwurah, to provide previously unavailable information about its new 'bucket'-led approach to funding research.

item.title: Government must take a stronger stance to disincentivise phone theft, Chair warns
item.description: The government has failed to act on using technological solutions to deter phone theft or hold the long-promised phone theft summit, a letter from the Home Secretary to the Chair of the Science, Innovation and Technology Committee reveals.

item.title: Costs of UK-US Pharma Deal will be met by DHSC, Science Minister confirms
item.description: The Science Minister, Lord Patrick Vallance, has provided further details of the recently announced UK-US pharmaceutical deal in a letter to the Science, Innovation and Technology Committee Chair, Dame Chi Onwurah.

item.title: MPs press government for clarity on the UK-US life sciences deal
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee Chair, Dame Chi Onwurah, has written to the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, Liz Kendall, seeking further details on the UK-US pharmaceutical trade deal.

item.title: Liz Kendall details government’s plans to tackle intimate AI deepfakes
item.description: The Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, Liz Kendall, has set out the UK Government’s plans to tackle the rapid rise of non-consensual AI deepfakes and AI ‘nudification’ tools.

item.title: Committee presses Government and Ofcom for details on action against AI intimate deepfakes
item.description: Dame Chi Onwurah, Chair of the Science, Innovation and Technology, has written to Ofcom and the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology seeking urgent clarification on its action to address intimate deepfakes generated by AI tools like Grok.

item.title: Life sciences: Science and Tech Committee presses government for details on US trade deal
item.description: The Science, Innovation and Technology Committee Chair, Dame Chi Onwurah, has written to Wes Streeting, Secretary of State for Health, and Liz Kendall, Secretary of State for Science and Technology, for clarity on the pharmaceutical trade deal with the US.

item.title: Government complacent over phone theft, fails to commit to long-promised summit with tech companies and police
item.description: Government complacent over phone theft, fails to commit to long-promised summit with tech companies and police
